serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of wheat germ is approximately 2 tablespoons (14 g), commonly sprinkled over yogurt, oatmeal, smoothies, or baked goods.

nutrient_source.title: Wheat germ, crude — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 169721)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros and micronutrients sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for crude wheat germ (FDC ID 169721). Singapore HPB values for energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, and cholesterol are consistent with USDA data and are used as the primary reference per HPB provenance in the input record; USDA used to supplement saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and micronutrient detail.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal) / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: Wheat germ GI of 15 (low) is reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2008 International Tables of GI and GL Values (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × available carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 15 × 4.6 / 100 ≈ 0.7, where available carbohydrate per 14 g serving = (32.7 − 20.1) × 0.14 ≈ 1.76 g net; using total carbohydrate 32.7 × 0.14 = 4.58 g per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (4 mg/100 g) and very high potassium (910 mg/100 g) with high dietary fibre (20.1 g/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Wheat germ is strongly supportive of blood pressure management due to its negligible sodium content, exceptionally high potassium-to-sodium ratio, and high fibre content consistent with DASH dietary principles; use 1–2 tablespoons daily as a food supplement.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI 15), very high dietary fibre (20.1 g/100 g), and high protein (20.6 g/100 g) attenuating postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Wheat germ has a low GI of 15 and very high fibre content that significantly slows glucose absorption; it is appropriate as a small daily supplement (1–2 tablespoons) for individuals with type 2 diabetes, though total carbohydrate intake should still be monitored within the overall meal plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (1.1 g/100 g), high polyunsaturated fat (3.7 g/100 g), high vitamin E (15.1 mg/100 g), high fibre (20.1 g/100 g), and zero cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Wheat germ supports cardiovascular health through its favourable fatty acid profile (low saturated fat, high PUFA), high vitamin E antioxidant content, zero cholesterol, and high soluble fibre that contributes to LDL cholesterol reduction; a daily supplement of 1–2 tablespoons is consistent with heart-healthy dietary patterns.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High vitamin E (15.1 mg/100 g) antioxidant activity, high fibre supporting gut immune modulation, very low saturated fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Wheat germ is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its high vitamin E content and anti-inflammatory micronutrient profile; however, patients with confirmed gluten sensitivity or coeliac disease must avoid wheat germ entirely.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wheat germ is the nutrient-dense embryo of the wheat kernel, separated during the milling of whole wheat into white flour. It appears as light golden flakes with a coarse, slightly crunchy texture and a mild, nutty flavour. Wheat germ is minimally processed — typically stabilised by light toasting to extend shelf life and prevent rancidity of its natural oils. It is one of the most concentrated plant-based sources of vitamin E (15.1 mg/100 g), folate (281 mcg DFE/100 g), zinc (12.3 mg/100 g), and selenium (79.2 mcg/100 g). It also provides exceptional dietary fibre (20.1 g/100 g), high-quality plant protein (20.6 g/100 g), and a favourable fatty acid profile dominated by polyunsaturated fats (3.7 g/100 g) with very low saturated fat (1.1 g/100 g). Its glycemic index is very low (GI 15), making it suitable as a supplement for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The very high potassium-to-sodium ratio (910 mg potassium vs 4 mg sodium per 100 g) makes it strongly supportive of blood pressure management. Wheat germ is widely used as a nutritional supplement sprinkled over yogurt, oatmeal, smoothies, salads, and baked goods. It is not suitable for individuals with coeliac disease or wheat/gluten allergy. It is plant-based, vegan, and halal-certified in most commercial forms.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced from Singapore HPB database (confidence 0.95 per input record) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 169721) for crude wheat germ. Micronutrient detail supplemented from USDA SR Legacy. GI value of 15 sourced from University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables. All values are consistent across sources.
notes: Cholesterol is confirmed zero per both HPB and USDA FoodData Central for wheat germ — naturally zero as this is a plant-derived food product. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g per USDA SR Legacy (confirmed zero). Vitamin C and vitamin A are 0.0 g per USDA SR Legacy (confirmed zero for crude wheat germ). Vitamin B12 and vitamin D are 0.0 per USDA SR Legacy (confirmed zero — plant-based food). Saturated fat (1.1 g/100 g), monounsaturated fat (0.9 g/100 g), polyunsaturated fat (3.7 g/100 g), and all micronutrient values supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 169721 to complete the nutrient profile beyond the HPB source. Wheat germ contains gluten and is not suitable for individuals with coeliac disease or wheat allergy. The food is not cuisine-specific; no mandatory multilingual local names apply (not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish). G6 multilingual compliance is satisfied as no mandatory local-language names are required for this globally generic ingredient.
